text: **The Four Treasuries, Subcategory Twelve** **Records of the Great Tang** **Category One: Miscellaneous Events** **Summary** We respectfully submit that the *Records of the Great Tang* is a single volume whose author's name is unknown. It records various events from the early Tang Dynasty to the reign of Emperor Yuanhe. Neither the bibliographies of the Tang nor the Song Dynasties list this work. It has a preface written by the author, who states that in the summer of the eighth year, he traveled south to the mountainous regions. On leisure days, while traveling by boat, he recorded what he heard. Upon examination, it is found that after the reign of Emperor Mu, only the reigns of Emperor Taizong, Emperor Dazong, and Emperor Xiantong had an eighth year. This book does not specify the era name, and it is also unclear when the eighth year refers to. The records of the deeds and words of Tang nobles and officials are quite detailed, and many of these records are used in historical works. Occasionally, it includes humorous anecdotes and trivial matters from court and common life, which often differ from other works. It mentions that during the reign of Emperor Chengyuan, Princesses Zhengguo and Haunguo were given posthumous titles, marking the beginning of such posthumous titles for princesses. However, it does not mention that Princess Pingyang, daughter of Emperor Gaozu, already had a posthumous title. Additionally, it recounts a story about Xiang Yingshi encountering an old man who said he resembled Prince Poyang. According to the *Book of Strange Stories*, the old man was actually a notorious thief, but here he is recorded as a strange person. This discrepancy arises because different versions were circulating at the time, and the author recorded what he heard, which inevitably led to contradictions. However, the parts that can be relied upon are about six or seven out of ten. This work was submitted for official review in June of the forty-fourth year of Emperor Qianlong's reign.

The Chief Editors: [Minister] Ji Yun, [Minister] Lu Xixiong, [Minister] Sun Shiyi The Chief Proofreader: [Minister] Lu Feicheng 钦定四库全书 大唐传载 《书》云：“不有博弈者乎？犹贤乎已。”这是圣人对饱食终日而无所作为的深恶痛绝。又说：“吾不试，故艺。”这里的“艺”不仅仅指多才多艺，而是因为这些技艺在当时没有实际用处，只能在困顿愁苦之时有所记录。八年夏天，南行至岭南，闲暇之时，乘船顺流而下，沿途听闻了许多故事，便将其记录下来，故名为《传载》。虽为小说，但或有可观看之处，让人或喜或笑。 杜河南（杜佑）常常收藏书籍达万卷之多，每卷之后必定有自题：“清俸买来手自校，汝曹读之知圣道。鬻及借人为不孝。” 阳道州（阳城）在担任朝官时，家境十分贫困，常将木枕和布被典当以换取钱财，许多人争相购买。 苏州开元寺东侧有一户陆姓人家世代居住，门前临河，有一块巨石矗立，相传是吴国陆绩担任郁林郡太守卸任后，因船只轻便，便用此石压重，人称“郁林石”。陆氏家族从陆绩到后代子孙，包括国朝太子少保兖公，都守护着这处居所。如今，子孙逐渐衰落，仅存十分之一。 费县西边有一片沼泽地，绵延十余里，每逢雨季，河水上涨，溢满沼泽，鱼虾丰富，人们依赖于此。到了白露时节，一夜之间，沼泽又会干涸如扫，真是奇异之事。

When Li Zhonggong was serving as chancellor, there was a bed for guarding food in the Hall of Government Affairs. Officials passed it on to move it, and the chancellor was not moved for fifty years. Li Zhonggong said, "This is a place where one discusses state affairs day and night. How can one allow rotten and worm-eaten things to remain without removing them? Superstitious sayings and taboos are not worth listening to." He was able to escape this and achieve his wish. He ordered it to be removed and burned, and the accumulated earth below was cleared away in fourteen baskets. Those who discussed it praised it as correct. Du Taibao, the Simple Duke, in the Dali era, had an old friend who left him a hundred pounds of gold. Thirty years later, he became the Military Governor of Huainan, and his son came to him to retrieve the gold, which was still sealed as before. In the Yuanhe era of the Zhaojun Three Ancestors, one person from each branch served as chancellor at the same time, all of whom were the third in line, namely, the Grand Tutor Jifu, the Grand Works Manager Jiang, and the Governor of Huazhou Fan. In the Tianbao era, there was a scholar who was traveling in Songzhou. At that time, Qian Gong Mian, who was young and poor, shared a room with the scholar. However, after ten days, the scholar fell ill and died. On his deathbed, he said to Qian, "I come from Hongzhou and was going to seek an official post in the Northern Capital. I fell ill here and am about to die. This is my fate. I am now giving you a hundred pounds of gold. My servants do not know about this. Please complete my funeral arrangements, and the remaining gold is yours." Li Gong agreed to handle the matter. After completing it, he secretly placed the gold in the tomb and buried it together with the scholar. Several years later, when Li Gong was serving as the Magistrate of Kaifeng, the scholar's brothers came with a document from Hongzhou, and it turned out that they were indeed searching for the scholar's remains. They went to Songzhou and found out that Li Gong had buried the scholar's remains.

Handling funeral affairs, the official specifically went to Kaifeng to inquire about the place where the gold came from. After taking leave, he went to the tomb, took out the gold, and handed it over. Wei Xiangong Xia Qing had the ability to discern people, but no one knew this. After dismissing from court, he met his second cousin Zhiyi, his cousin Qu Mou, and Sheng in the street. All were of the twenty-fourth rank and served as officials. They gathered around their horses for a long time. Xia Qing said, "Today we met three officials of the twenty-fourth rank, let's give them titles." Zhiyi said, "You will definitely become a prime minister, take good care of your position." He told Qu Mou, "You will receive special favor and quickly become a high-ranking official." He told Sheng, "Among the three, you will have the longest career and reach the highest rank." Later, it turned out as he said. Du Ya, serving in Huainan, spent millions of coins on dragon boat races, lotus picking, and other elaborate games. Yu Di, serving in Xiangzhou, lit mountain lanterns that consumed two thousand stones of oil. Li Changfang, serving in Jingnan, went hunting and spent lavishly on decorations. His wife Dugu also had a female troupe of two thousand people, all wearing red and purple embroidered coats. These three regions also suffered from such extravagance. Nanyang De Shi, the son of the former Gingshi Gao, once bought the garden land of Lou Shide in Dongdu and built a study hall. People in Luoyang said, "What used to be Lou Shide's garden is now De Shi's study hall." On the fifth day of the tenth month of the first year of Xingyuan, the emperor first ordered the eunuch Dou Wenzhang to supervise the left wing of the Divine Protection Army. Ma Youlin was appointed as the Left General of the Divine Protection Army, marking the beginning of the Divine Strategy Army's supervision of the military. On the twenty-fifth day of the sixth month of the twelfth year of Zhenyuan, the emperor first appointed Dou Wenzhang as the Left Chief of the Divine Protection Army and Huo Xianming as the Right Chief of the Divine Protection Army. On the same day, Zhang Shangjin was appointed as the Left and Right Chief of the Divine Martial Army.

During the early years of the Jianzhong era, Guan Bo served as a high-ranking official and proposed using literati to manage the low-ranking clerks and officials who were causing harm. The Yang family of Hongnong, residing in the Eastern Capital, claimed descent from four high-ranking military officials and were said to have inherited a jade ring held by a yellow bird, which was taken away by Yang Guozhong during the Tianbao era, and its current whereabouts are unknown. Zhang Shougui, a native of Pinglu in Shaanxi, traveled from Youzhou to pay a formal visit to the emperor and passed through his hometown, where he expressed filial piety to the magistrate Li Hang and witnessed the magistrate of Shaanxi, Li Mian, being shackled and the crowd crying out in injustice. Zhang Shougui said, "How can I help you in such a difficult situation?" Upon reaching Lingbao, he immediately reported to the judge of Yanzhou, who later became a chancellor during the Zhenguan era. Zhang Maocong's daughter, Princess Yizhang, was bestowed the title of Princess of Zheng and posthumously honored as Zhuangmu. Wei You's daughter, Princess Tang'an, was bestowed the title of Princess of Han and posthumously honored as Zhenmu. The relevant authorities chose a date to confer these titles, and since the founding of the dynasty, princesses had been posthumously titled, but no additional posthumous titles had been added until now. The posthumous titles for princesses began from this point onward. Xu Shangshu and Shen Libu were both skilled in their respective fields—Xu was fond of drinking, and Shen was skilled in eating. Yang Dongchuan's descendant once said, "Xu's lungs and Shen's spleen truly bring peace." There was a scholar who loved eating boiled beef head and one day suddenly dreamed of his death and being arrested and taken to the underworld. There was a bull's head beside him, and he had no fear, so he touched the bull's head and said, "This head is great for boiling." The bull's head person laughed and let him return. During the Yuanhe era, several officials in the palace drank and talked about their lives, their loves and dislikes, and their fears. Some said they loved paintings and games but feared lies and flattery. The official from Runan, Zhou Yuan, alone said he loved the governor of Xuanzhou and feared tigers.

During the reign of Emperor Dezong in the Zhenyuan era, there was a prohibition against unmarried officials riding large horses. Someone reported to the ruling officials that there were many large horses, and due to the strict prohibition, even those with the means could not ride them, suggesting that the horses might all end up being taken by the powerful military governors in the region. Lu Hongjian, who loved tea, compiled the *Cha Jing* (Tea Classic) in three volumes, which became widely circulated. It was common to see tea shops where clay figures of tea gods were created and placed near stoves and pots. These figures were considered tea deities, and people would offer tea to them during transactions, or use hot water to honor them otherwise. During the reign of Emperor Gaozu, military leaders who were granted the title of "Grand Commander" (大总管) and bestowing them with a ceremonial staff (旌节) for special missions. For defensive purposes, military leaders were granted the title of "Grand Marshal" (大都督). In the early years of Emperor Xuanzong's reign, the practice of granting the title of "Ambassador of the Circuit" (节度大使) to princes and the establishment of the "Imperial Military Command" (元帅府) by the Crown Prince was implemented. The practice of chancellors holding the position of Circuit Governor (节度使) began with Niu Xianke. In the ninth year of Emperor Xuanzong's reign, the Imperial Clan Bureau (宗正寺) was ordered to appoint all officials from among the royal family members. The Tang Dynasty's household registration system categorized men as follows: newborns were registered as "yellow" (黄), four-year-olds as "young" (小), sixteen-year-olds as "middle-aged" (中), twenty-year-olds as "adults" (丁), and sixty-year-olds as "elderly" (老). The four types of taxes and labor services were: rent (租), tax (税), corvée (役), and public works (徭). Adult males were required to pay two stones of grain per year, and the amount of silk and other goods varied depending on the local produce. They were also required to perform twenty days of labor service per year, with an additional two days added in leap years. In the first year of the Zhide era, Wen Shuqing was appointed as a special envoy (宣谕使), allowing people to pay money to receive official positions or pass the *Mingjing* examination. In the second year of the Zhide era, a decree was issued allowing monks and Taoist priests to pay money to be ordained, with varying amounts. In the first year of the Qianyuan era, the coinage reform involved the creation of new *Qianyuan* coins, each weighing twenty pounds and valued at fifty. In the first year of the Baoying era, due to rampant counterfeiting and rising prices, the value of the coins was reduced, and they were valued at one for thirty.

In the second year of the Qianyuan era, Yuan Zai, the Vice-Chancellor of the Censorate, served as the Tax Commissioner for the Jianghuai region, setting a fixed quota for high-ranking households and levying a tax known as "bai zhuo" (white wearing) and "gu" (alcohol sales). In the second year of the Zhude era, an imperial edict was issued to all prefectures and counties to set a quota for the sale of alcohol and to collect taxes on a monthly basis. In the second year of the Jianzhong era, the "qing mia" (early spring) tax was further increased. In the middle of the Daye era, the initial tax rate was ten coins per mu of land, which was increased by five coins in the third year. An imperial edict appointed a Vice-Chancellor to carry out the mission, and later, the responsibilities were transferred to the Ministry of Finance. Lü Zhongcheng Mai, the Vice-Chancellor, had four valuable qin (zither) instruments, each worth tens of thousands of coins. He also had a cold jade ancient chime known as "Xiangquan Hezhi" (Harmonious Intention of Flowing Water). Li Henan Su, who replaced Du Gong Jianshi, and Han Lishi Yu, the Minister of Personnel, served as the prefect of Henan. When they returned to the capital, they asked each other about the administration of their respective regions. Li replied, "Jianshi is better than Su." During the time of Li Xiangguo Cheng, the Chancellor, Yan Xiuyu were both in the southern province. There was a vacancy in the position of magistrate of Wannian, and many people recommended Yan. Li said, "The two Yans are not as good as Yu." Doulu Shu, the Minister of Justice, was a young official traveling in Quzhou. He dreamed of an elderly man who said, "In twenty years, you will be the governor of this prefecture." Indeed, he later became the governor of Quzhou and built a pavilion at the site of his dream, naming it "Zheng Meng Ting" (Pavilion of the Dream). Zheng Chuzhou Lu visited Qujiang and saw a local official drunk by the poolside. The official said, "If I were to be promoted, I would enter the ranks of the bureaucracy." Bai Bingshi Juyi said, "In Zhongzhou, there is one tree of litchi and one tree of wutong. To the south of Zhongzhou, there are no wutong trees, and to the north, there are no litchi trees." Jia Zhichangshi, the Grand Secretary, was a lifelong critic of Buddhism. Once, while taking a brief nap during his duties, he suddenly saw a person with a bull's head, no more than a foot tall, carrying a small pot and burning firewood in front of his bed. The official was startled and asked, "Who are you?" The apparition replied, "This is the boiling cauldron, punishing those who criticize Buddhism." The official said, "What is a small ghost to fear?" He then extended his foot under the bed, and the boiling water suddenly splashed onto his foot, rising up and scalding him to death.

In the area of Wujiang, there was a tree known as the "Wuzhu" tree, which was associated with Xiang Yu and had remained lush and flourishing for over a thousand years. During the reign of Emperor Dezong (建中年), the county magistrate Zhang Qin was displeased with visitors admiring the tree and ordered it to be cut down. To this day, the spot where the tree once stood remains barren and does not grow grass. Li Zhen'ao, the father of Zhao Gongqiao, served as the magistrate of Qizhou in Zizhou Prefecture. In a letter to a friend, he humorously remarked that the naming conventions of places (prefectures and counties) were not under his control, as they seemed to be influenced by the names of his family members. He wrote, "The prefecture bears the name of my son, and the county bears the name of my wife. This is not due to any influence of mine, but rather it seems to be a matter of fate." During the Kaiyuan era, Liu Juling served as the governor of Guangzhou, and his brother Zhongqiu was a scholar at the Lize Hall. They were very close and shared a secret elixir, which was divided precisely. A Daoist practitioner named Luo Fu prepared the elixir for Liu Juling and divided it into two equal parts, using a knife to ensure that the division was exact to the smallest degree. Xiang Gongcao Ying Shi and Zhao Yuwai Ji, two local officials during the Kaiyuan era, lived together in Xingjing Lane and shared a single pair of shoes. Over time, they noticed that the shoes had worn down on one side, leading to a humorous exchange. Zhao commented, "This shows that we are destined to travel long distances." Xiang replied, "Perhaps our fortunes lie in the paths we walk." Fang Taifu, a high-ranking official, once said that one cannot truly appreciate the beauty of the mountains and waters without visiting Xingde Temple and Dongting Lake in Suzhou and Hangzhou. He emphasized the importance of experiencing these natural wonders firsthand. Shou'an County is known for its natural beauty, particularly the "Pengyu Spring" and the stone stream. During the Zhenyuan era, Li Bingkong served as the magistrate of Shou'an County and cleared the overgrown vegetation along the paths, making them accessible to visitors. By the early years of the Tahe era, Cui Hui, a local official, marked the paths for visitors, and people began to visit and explore the area. Yan Zhenqing, a famous calligrapher and statesman of the Tang dynasty, inscribed his name on stones, either placing them on high mountains or sinking them at the bottom of large rivers. He reflected on the impermanence of human endeavors, saying, "Who knows if the hills and valleys will not change over time?"

In his later years, Du Gu Changzhou had a particular fondness for playing the zither. Despite suffering from an eye disease, he paid no heed and desired to focus solely on listening to the music. In front of the Confucian temple in Qufu, there were several ancient cypress trees, said to be over a thousand years old, each with a circumference of ten times that of a human arm. Pan Hua, impoverished while serving as the military governor of Yan, could not establish trust with people from all directions. He then ordered the cypress trees to be felled, cutting them into writing materials and carving them into utensils to distribute as gifts. Zhang Wen Zhen Gong's daughter, who had married into the Lu family, once sought an official position for her uncle. She waited for the official announcement before asking about it. The official remained silent but pointed to a support bed turtle and showed it to her. The daughter bowed and returned to her room, telling her husband, "Uncle has obtained the official position." During the Eastern Pilgrimage of the Kaiyuan era, an elderly man from Taiyuan named Yu Boliang, who was 128 years old, had a clear and bright mind despite his advanced age. His son had already passed away, and his two grandsons, aged in their seventies and eighties, accompanied him. He traveled north to the imperial court, where he was introduced to the emperor. The elderly man did not perform the bowing ritual. Yu Boliang said, "I was a minister of Emperor Taizong of Tang. Through the passage of time, I have come to serve Your Majesty again, which is my honor." The emperor smiled and pitied him, bestowing upon him purple robes, ivory tablets, and additional honors. Yu Boliang recounted that he was born in the year of Ren Shou during the Sui Dynasty and could clearly remember events from the end of the Daye era. Li You Cheng Yi, at the age of 29, served as the Right Chancellor. By the age of 59, he held the same position again. Yu Liang Shi, who served Zhang Xuzhou Jianfeng, only mentioned his surname as Xin Qiu Du. Xiao Gong Cao Ying Shi, while traveling along the Bashi Bridge, met an elderly man with white hair and an unusual appearance. Xiao found this very strange and looked around. The elderly man, noticing Xiao, asked him about it. The elderly man said, "You resemble my deceased friend." Xiao insisted on hearing more. The elderly man replied, "I was friendly with Prince Po Yang Wang Huai. You bear a striking resemblance to him. You are the sixth-generation ancestor of Xiao Ying Shi." When Xiao asked where he came from, the elderly man did not respond and left.

### Translation The Jin Valley in Luoyang is twenty-five li away from the city. During the Western Jin Dynasty, Shi Chong built a garden along the Jin Valley, with tall buildings and pavilions. The remaining ruins are rugged and uneven, but there is a large black locust tree that still stands lush and flourishing. Zheng Gong, during the early Tang Dynasty, served as a palace censor and was appointed as the manager of the postal service. He ordered that milestones be placed at each inn, a practice that began with him. During the mid-Tang Dynasty, a northern person served as the local official in Jintan County, Runzhou. He brought over ten scorpions in a bamboo tube and placed them behind a willow tree in the official residence. The scorpions multiplied to over a hundred, but due to the local climate, they could not sting people. The southern residents did not recognize them and called them "the official's scorpions." During the early Tang Dynasty, the announcement of the Jinshi (imperial examination) results was made at the Shangshu Province. All those who passed the preliminary exams gathered at the main hall, and the results were announced there. Those who did not pass, wearing hats in pairs or groups of three, waited until dusk to hear a single chant. All the bamboo and silk documents in Chang'an withered and died, a metaphor for the loss of knowledge or the end of an era.

During the Kaiyuan era, a high-ranking official of the Ministry of Personnel was requested by Prince Ning to recommend ten relatives for official positions. The official then went to see the prince and asked, "Among the ten, is there anyone who could be considered for promotion?" The prince replied, "Nine of them are not suitable, but one person, whose name I will not mention, can be recommended to the Ministry of Personnel. The other nine will be promoted to excellent positions, but the one I mentioned will be allowed to pass the winter assembly." In the county of Kunshan, there is a pond known as the "Jade Ruler Pond," which was formed during the Dali era when a village girl, who was the first wife of the crown prince, had her jade ruler turn into a dragon. In the eastern part of Luoyang, the Longmen Xiangshan Temple was named the "Spring Palace" during the reign of Empress Wu Zetian. She often held court from a stone pavilion, with all officials and ministers gathered outside. During the Yonghe era, an official responsible for religious ceremonies at the Imperial Music Bureau failed to attend a ritual at the Altar of Heaven, claiming that the temple was actually a Buddhist monastery and that he, as a small monk, was not obligated to attend. The sandy embankment was constructed in the third year of the Tianbao era, at the request of Xiao Jingzhong, who built a covered path along important roads to facilitate the passage of carriages and horses, with sand covering the path. During the Tianbao era, musical compositions were named after border regions, such as Liangzhou, Ganzhou, and Yizhou. These compositions, once popular, eventually faded as the regions they represented were lost to the Western Turks. In GaoPing, Xu Hongyi, an official responsible for investigating corruption, created a new position called the "Inspector of Court Etiquette." This official was tasked with maintaining order at the main gate of the imperial palace and reporting any misconduct to the court. When asked by a high-ranking official why this was necessary, Xu replied, "I am replying on behalf of the court officials. The reason for this is to prevent disrespect to the emperor."

Pei Shuye, upon entering his official career at the age of twenty, always wore a headband (巾子) in a traditional style, never following the fashions of the time. Despite changes in fashion over several generations, the style he wore at the age of ninety was the same as when he was young. Today, the headband style worn by Pei is known as the "Pei style." Han Taibao, who was born with a natural understanding of music, once watched a guest play the qin and stopped to admire it, exclaiming, "Marvelous! This is the piece 'Guangling San' composed by Ji Kang!" The piece reflects the transition period between the Wei and Jin dynasties, sharing the same origin as "Guangling San" but differing in some aspects. The musical tone is associated with the autumn season, symbolizing the decline and end of the year. This is why it reflects the decline of the Wei dynasty. The music implies a weakening of the ruler's authority, possibly foreshadowing a change in power. This is why it predicts the rise of the Sima family and their eventual usurpation of power. "Guangling" refers to the region of Yangzhou, and "San" (散) means dispersion or wandering. "Yang" is the surname of Empress Yang, who, along with her father, contributed to the downfall of the Jin dynasty. The music reflects the sorrow and pain of the Jin dynasty's fall, particularly during the Yongjia Rebellion. Ji Kang composed the piece to pass it on to future generations and to avoid the political turmoil of his time by attributing it to supernatural beings. Han Taibao often said that among the five blessings in the *Great Plan* (洪范), wealth is the closest to danger, and one should not seek it through unethical means. Li Xiping, a general, once visited Sang Daomao and was told he would become the governor of Jingzhao (京兆尹) in the future, but should be careful not to kill too many people. Li Xiping responded that a warrior would not have such aspirations. After the Xingyuan Rebellion, Li Xiping became the governor of Jingzhao, and Sang Daomao, who was in captivity, reminded him of his earlier advice.

Han Taibao, when serving as the Chief Inspector and Prefect of Jingzhao, always presented matters in the inner palace before the court, never requesting a private audience in the secondary palace. The emperor once said to him, "I cannot fully discuss matters with you here; you should come to the Yan Ying Hall to deliberate on major state affairs, which will be more beneficial." His friends all advised him, "Since the reign of Emperor Suzong, all officials have presented matters in the Yan Ying Hall, allowing for detailed discussions. Why do you alone present matters in the outer court before the officials, and how can you ensure they are handled discreetly?" Han Taibao replied, "The role of the censor is to maintain balance in the world, to break down the strong and straighten the crooked. How can people know about it if it is done in secret? How can one request a private audience to speak secretly, acting in private interest rather than for the state? The Yan Ying Hall was established by Emperor Suzong because of the difficulty in walking of the elderly official Miao Jinqing. Later officials who presented matters in the secondary palace often sought private favor and protection, hoping to gain the emperor's favor and seek the position of chancellor. How can one aspire to such things?" Zhang Mijuan, from the time he began his official career until he became the Secretary of the Imperial Secretariat, always held diplomatic missions and traveled to the border regions three times, ultimately dying outside the border at Chilin. Wei Yizhou, from a young age, did not like to hear about the southern provinces. When he was appointed as chancellor, he went out of his residence and saw a map of a state or prefecture, hesitated, and did not dare to look at it for a long time. As he was about to leave, he mistakenly looked at it and found it to be a map of Yazhou. He was eventually demoted and ended his career in disgrace. Wang Huainan, when serving as the Right Governor, someone reported to him, "You have been appointed as the Right Governor." Wang replied, "If I hold this position, I will be concerned that people will call me 'Wang, who does not understand poetry,' and I will be known as the Right Vice-Chancellor." Yang Daozhou, in Yangzhou, never accumulated wealth; he only kept the necessities for his daily use. Guests who praised the quality of his money and silk would often receive them. There was a person named Chen Chang who would wait until the beginning of the month to receive his salary and then go to praise the quality of the money and silk, always gaining something each month.

The Chancellor of the Middle Secretariat, originally from Kaizhou, often had two Jinshi (imperial scholars) named Li Tong and Cui Chong visit him, staying for over a month. Once, there was a guest from Western Sichuan, a military officer who claimed to have divination skills, and he said at a gathering that Li Tong would face a tiger-related misfortune within three days. Three days later, the diviner and his guests visited a mountain temple, traveling from the upper to the lower part of the temple as evening approached. Li Tong descended first, followed by Cui Chong, who shouted, "Wait for me, wait for me." Li Tong mistook Cui's shout for a tiger's roar, causing him to lose his balance and fall, resulting in a serious injury from which he recovered after several days. When the military officer returned, he told Li Tong that his misfortune had passed. During the late Jianzhong period, Su Geishi (an official named Su Dai) was appointed as a Taichang Doctor. He encountered the rebellion led by Zhu Ci and was heading to the imperial residence. At night, he traveled through mountains and valleys with two candles guiding him, which disappeared once he had passed the danger. The text questions whether this was due to his loyalty and anger. During the Kaiyuan period, the brothers Li Guo, Li Peng, and Li He were all renowned for their talents. Li He was particularly famous for his poetry, especially his ode to the city of Weicheng. Li Peng was skilled in dance, and Li Guo was adept at playing the jiaogǔ (a type of drum). Emperor Xuanzong asked Li Guo how many times he had beaten the drum, and Li replied that he had beaten it 5,000 times. The emperor then said that he had beaten it even more, to the point of destroying three cabinets. Several years later, someone heard Li Guo beat the drum once more, and as a result, he was given a special drumstick. The jiaogǔ was later passed down to a disciple, who was serving as a governor during the Jianzhong period. The governor then took a lacquered tray from Jingling, poured water onto the jiaogǔ, and it remained undisturbed throughout the day, indicating its perfection. The text also mentions that the skill of playing the jiaogǔ lies in the proper tuning and slowness. After one tuning, it could remain in perfect condition for a month. However, it is now unknown where it is. People in the northern gate of Anyi County reported that there was a scorpion as large as a pipa (a musical instrument). Whenever it appeared, it did not sting people, leading them to believe it was a sacred creature that had been sealed away for many years.

During the late reign of Emperor Dezong and the early reign of Emperor Xianzong, the general Hao Pin repeatedly captured and killed the Western barbarians in Jingzhou and built the city of Linjing, which the Western barbarians feared. The Tibetan king cast a golden plaque with the name "Hao Pin" and decreed that whoever could capture Hao Pin would be rewarded with the golden plaque. During the Jianzhong period, Li Xilie attacked the city of Bianzhou. The city defenses used civilians, women, and supplies to fill the trenches, which were called "wet ditches." When Dou Can became chancellor, he used his cousin's younger brother, Shen, as his eyes and ears. Every time he appointed an official, he first told Shen, and people called Dou Can, the Censor-General, "the magpie." In the Táng Nǚ Temple in Tóngzhōu, there was a thief named Dong Taigong who had a painted back; when other thieves came, they all came to pay homage and wish him well. Now the painted feet are all mouths and teeth. The Hán Gāo Temple in Xiāngzhōu originally commemorated the act of untying the jade pendant from the Han dynasty, but now it is mistakenly attributed to Emperor Gaozu of Han. In the main hall of the Pínglù County Magistrate's office in Xiànzhōu, there is a beam in the western sequence with inscriptions by Sui Dynasty officials Fang Gong and Du Gong in the twelfth month of the ninth year of Renshou. Xuanling and Ruhui inscribed their names here; Fang was 23 years old and Du was 26 years old. Now it has been moved to the beam in the dining hall of the provincial governor's office.

The two brothers of Yang Jingzhao were both skilled in literature and extremely diligent, sometimes collaborating on a single piece while sitting together on courtyard stones, with frost accumulating on their sleeves. They would only stop when their work was completed. The nine brothers of Li Yuan, the Duke of Wei, had four of them holding land. Li Yuan served as the governor of Xia Prefecture, and his brothers held the positions of military governors in Xu, Si, Fengxiang, Xuanchu, and Hezhong. Li Xian served as the governor of Jiangxi and the military governor of Lingnan. Li Su served as the military governor of Tang, Deng, Xiangyang, Xu, Si, Fengxiang, Zelü, and Weibo. Li Ting served as the military governor of Xia, Lingwu, Hedong, Zheng, Huai, Weibo, Fenni, and Fengxiang. All of them received their commands on the same platform, and there was none like them. In the 15th year of Yuanhe, Xin Qiu, Du Shu, and Du Yuanying served as censors and imperial secretaries, taking turns on duty. By custom, they would often recite, "After thirty years of service, my hair is still white and my face is still green." At dusk, they would lean against the red gate, their faces flushed with red sweat, and they would submit a memorial to the throne, asking for a reward. In Guangwu Mountain, Henan, there is a flowing osmanthus spring. Shi Siming established a temple to Han Gaozu on it. At the foot of Mao Mountain, there is a palace where the Lord of Mao refined elixirs; the well is fragrant and pure, untouched by impurities. Once, a worker repairing the palace learned that if he used the water to cook meat, it would not cook for a long time. During the Baoli era, there was a claim in Bozhou that a holy water could cure chronic illnesses, but no one got better. From Luoyang to Jiangxi and several other prefectures, people competed to offer gold, goods, and clothing to drink the water, hoping to gain a fortune, and thousands were deceived. Li Zanhuang, the Duke of Wei, ordered a public gathering in Zhejiang to test the water. He placed gold in the market and had the water tested by boiling five pounds of pork; if it were holy water, the meat should remain unchanged. After a short time, the meat was cooked thoroughly, and the people's doubts were dispelled. The fraudsters were soon exposed.

Wuling Mountain in Lang Prefecture is said to be a place of transformation, where every year during heavy rainstorms, new things appear on the steep cliffs. Changping in Ze Prefecture is the site where General Bai Qi massacred the soldiers of the State of Zhao during the Warring States period. During the Kaiyuan era, the emperor decreed this area to be a place of mercy and remembrance for the victims. Today, the area remains shrouded in mist, and there is a red spring below with a stone tablet inscribed "Dan Well." In the southern part of Deqing County, Huzhou Prefecture, there is Qianxi Village, which was once a place where music and dance were taught in the past. Even today, several hundred families in the village still practice music, and many of the performers of music and dance in southern China come from here, hence the saying "Dance from Qianxi." Zhongnan Mountain has a pool that was originally in Xianyang but suddenly flew away overnight. Wherever it flew, it brought heavy rain and fish. It eventually settled in the middle peak of Zhongnan Mountain, where the water is several feet deep, over a mile wide, and dark blue-black. Clouds and rain often emerge from the pool. During droughts, prayers are answered. Monks who gather firewood sometimes see groups of dragons splashing in the water. Qing Mountain in Zhaoying flew from Hebei to Chang'an. At night, it passed by, and the sound of thunder was like a strong wind, with earth and stones falling randomly. It fell directly into a village of about a hundred families west of Xin Feng, where the villagers used the mountain as a cemetery. Now, a temple named Chiguosi has been built on the mountain. During the Tang Dynasty, Wei Qizhong visited Zhang Jingzang when he was young. Zhang Jingzang treated him poorly and did not answer his questions about fortune and misfortune. Wei Qizhong became angry and said, "I traveled thousands of miles with provisions to see you, not just for a casual visit. I expected you to teach me something, but you remain silent, showing no earnestness. Why is that? Moreover, whether one is successful or poor, rich or lowly, is determined by fate, not by you. Why should you care?" He then left angrily. Zhang Jingzang quickly stood up and said, "Your fortune and position are determined in your anger. You will eventually reach the highest rank." During the An Lushan Rebellion, the salt pool in Jie County suddenly became fresh, but after the rebellion was quelled, it returned to its original state.

At the Chongsheng Temple in the Upper Capital, there was a makeup chamber belonging to Princess Xu Xianfei. Emperor Taizu once summoned the princess, but she did not arrive for a long time, and he became angry with her. Therefore, he composed a poem saying: "In the morning she came to the mirror stand, after finishing her makeup, she hesitated for a moment. A thousand gold coins would only bring a smile; how could she come just because she was summoned?" Prince Yu of Han served as Minister of Rites. One morning, he heard someone playing the flute in Yongxing Lane and asked if the player was a musician from the Ministry of Rites. The answer was yes. Thereafter, whenever he heard music, he would beat the musician and ask, "How could you sleep and play the flute on a certain day?" He also saw Kang Kunlun playing the pipa and remarked, "The 'pi' sound is more than the 'pa' sound, and it is not yet possible to play the fifty-four strings of the main string. From bottom to top, it is called 'pi,' and from top to bottom, it is called 'pa.'" Pei Yanling once became angry with Li Jingzhao Chong and said, "Recently, you have allowed your beard and mustache to turn white, still pretending to be behind people." Xue Rudi lived in Nanyue and often associated with a monk. This monk annually offered incense to the Buddha from his topknot until he was over eighty years old. After his death, a year later, a man from the state gave birth to a son with an extraordinary appearance and a very fragrant topknot. People admired him from ten steps away. The child did not eat fish or meat and, after several years, became a high-ranking monk in Nanyue, specializing in monastic rules. Wang Zao and Wang Su both took the imperial examination during the Zhenyuan period and ranked fourteenth. Whenever they visited a well-known family, they were addressed as "Wang the Fourteenth." Sometimes, when asked who they were, they would say, "I am Wang Zao" or "I am Wang Su." Chang Xianggun served as a judge of miscellaneous writings at the Ministry of Rites. After the examination, he announced: "In the morning sun, he entered the examination hall, his thoughts were not sharp, through the night, he stopped writing, he regretted having more than enough. Therefore, those who passed the miscellaneous writing examination usually did not exceed one hundred people. Bao Jiuji Fang, as a high-ranking official at the Ministry of Rites, also failed many people in the recitation test, and it was called 'Chang's miscellaneous writings and Bao's recitation test.'" The Attendant-in-Ordinary and the Censor-in-Ordinary shared the same kitchen. A kitchen worker presented fresh mushrooms to the censor and asked, "Are they poisonous?" The kitchen worker replied, "The Attendant-in-Ordinary has already tasted them."

In the early years of the Gan Yuan period, the mother of Li Xu, who held the position of Left Counselor to the Crown Prince, Zhang, was eighty-eight years old. Because Li Xu had been away for a long time, she grew increasingly concerned about him. Suddenly, she felt milk flowing from her left breast and looked at her grandson, saying, "Your father is coming!" Within ten days, Li Xu returned. During the early years of the Zhi De period, during the An Lushan Rebellion, there was a severe famine in the Hedong region. Suddenly, on a barren land spanning fifteen li, beans and grains grew, were harvested, and regrew again. Approximately five or six thousand stones of rice were obtained, which was very fine and round, benefiting the people greatly. During the Kai Yuan and Tian Bao periods, the Cui family was known for their strict adherence to family traditions and rules. The teacher said that the southeastern part of Yongning Lane in Chang'an was known as the "Jin Zhan" area, and the western part of Anyi Lane was known as the "Yu Zhan" area. Yongning was the land of Wang Tai Fu E, and Anyi was the land of Ma Beiping Sui. Later, both Wang and Ma's lands were granted to officials such as Han Linghong, Shi Xiancheng, and Li Daiyi, known as the "Jin Zhan" area being broken up. Ma Sui's garden was known as the "Yu Zhan" area, which was broken but not completely. During the time when Chang Xianggong (Chang Jun) was serving as the governor of Fujian, there was a monk skilled in divination and predicting future events with great accuracy. He was concerned about the old monk and ordered his disciples to learn his techniques. The monk said, "This skill has a natural talent and cannot be learned hastily." Once, he saw someone who could be taught among the attendants of the chancellor and summoned him. The monk placed five-colored threads on a rack in a dark room and instructed him to look at them, saying, "People use their eyesight, but it is not enough. Train and practice diligently." After a month, he could vaguely recognize the white ones. After half a year, he could suddenly understand the colors clearly. The monk named this technique "Seeing Colors in Darkness and Daylight." He also passed down the method and said that future generations would not surpass him. Li Zhonggong (Li Jifu) said that Huang Che's divination skills were second only to Yuan Xu.

Liu Yuxi, the Minister of Rites, lived with a friend for three years. The friend remarked that he had never seen Liu speak harsh words. Tang Lin was known for his kindness and forgiveness. When he wanted to attend a funeral, he ordered his servant to fetch a white robe. The servant mistakenly brought another garment and, fearing retribution, did not dare to present it. During the funeral, Tang Lin said to him, "Today the weather is unfavorable for mourning and weeping. Let us stop and put on the white robe instead." He then ordered the servant to prepare medicine, but it was not cooked well. Tang Lin noticed the mistake but said, "Today the weather is overcast and unsuitable for taking medicine. Let us discard it." He never exposed the servant's error. Tang Jiao, during the Zhenguan era, served as the head of the Ministry of Personnel. Before him, candidates were immediately filled as vacancies arose. Tang Jiao first proposed to gather all candidates in winter and complete the process by late spring. This practice is still followed today. Xu You, a judge during the Tang Dynasty, governed with leniency and benevolence. Officials were grateful for his kindness and trust. They agreed among themselves to say, "If anyone is punished by a cane from Judge Xu, they will be dismissed." He never punished anyone with a cane during his entire tenure. Yan Zhenqing, a famous calligrapher and statesman during the Tang Dynasty, served as a Censorial Inspector and was appointed as the military commander for the troops in the Hexi and Longyou regions. There was a wrongful case in Wuyuan, which was resolved, and it rained. The people of the county called it "the rain of the Censorial Inspector."

Emperor Dezong asked Li Qian, "People say Lu Qi is a traitor and villain. Why is that so?" Li Qian replied, "Everyone knows it, but only you do not know. This is why he is considered a traitor and villain." When Li Xieli was guarding Caozhou, Lu Qi, as the prime minister, recommended Yan Zhenqing to go and announce the imperial decree to him. He told Yan, "Uncle Yan, this mission comes from the emperor's own will." Yan replied, "Your Excellency, the blood on the face of the former Vice-Chancellor was my own tongue that I licked to stop the bleeding. How could I dare to use my dying years to tempt a tiger's mouth?" Upon hearing this, Lu Qi wept. Lu Qi was indeed the son of the Vice-Chancellor Yi. The Qingping officials of the southern barbarians are like the prime ministers of our country. During the Yuanhe era, Deng Pang came to the court. The prime minister asked him, "What does your name, 'Pang,' mean?" Deng replied, "It is similar to the name 'Zongjing' of the great court." Su Bu and Liu Changshi, both officials, had collected books up to two million volumes. In Henan, Feng Su had three sons: Tao, Ju, and Tu. They all passed the imperial examinations consecutively and achieved success in the macrology category, which was an unprecedented achievement in their generation. During the Tahe era, the Feng family had eighteen members who passed the imperial examinations, and eight of them were brothers and nephews. Li Cheng, the prime minister, was a翰林学士 who used the position of the sun relative to the steps as a way to enter the waiting area. He was lazy and always stepped over eight bricks when entering, hence he was called the "Eight Brick Scholar." Longxing Temple in Yongzhou was the former residence of Wu Jun's military司马. The monk Huaisu was skilled in cursive and regular script. Once, while dredging a well, he found a seal of a military司马. The text on the seal was still clear and new. Huaisu used this seal for his calligraphy as a mark. Yinggong, despite his high position as a仆射, always personally prepared porridge for his sister when she was ill. Once, he burned his mustache while preparing the porridge. His sister said, "There are many servants and maids. Why do you trouble yourself so?" Yinggong replied, "Is there no one else? But now that my sister is old, and I am old too, even if I wanted to continue making porridge for her, could I still do so?"

Wei Qing once said: "When I was twelve or thirteen years old, I was a disreputable bandit who killed anyone I encountered. By the time I was fourteen or fifteen, I was a difficult-to-resist bandit who killed anyone who displeased me. By the time I was seventeen or eighteen, I was a good bandit who fought on the battlefield and killed people. By the time I was twenty, I had become a great general of the empire, using my military skills to save lives." Wei Qing was known for his broad-mindedness and his exceptional skill in avoiding spears. Whenever he entered the battlefield, enemies who tried to spear him could not hit him, and he would often take their spears and use them to spear the enemies himself. When Yuanji heard about this, he did not believe it. He ordered the spears to be stripped of their blades and tried to test Wei Qing. Wei Qing said, "Even if the blade is sharp, I am not afraid of being injured." Yuanji tried to spear him multiple times, but he could not hit him, and Wei Qing took all the spears from him. Yuanji, who was strong enough to fight ten men, was greatly humiliated and resentful. Emperor Taizong, facing Duan Jiande, said to Wei Qing, "I will use a bow and arrow, and you will use a long spear. Even a million people would be no match for us." They then galloped to the enemy camp, knocked on the gate, and shouted, "The Great Emperor of the Tang Dynasty, Qin Wang, can match anyone. Come and fight me!" Despite many pursuing riders, they could not catch up. During the battle against Duan Jiande, before the battle began, Emperor Taizong saw a young man riding a gray horse with bright armor. He pointed to the young man and said to Wei Qing, "That horse he is riding is truly a fine horse." Wei Qing kept saying so. He then asked for permission to capture the young man. The emperor said, "If you underestimate your enemy, you will lose. If you lose even a horse, it is not what I wish for. Please assess the situation and attack only if you are certain of success." Wei Qing then galloped towards the young man and captured him. It turned out that the young man was Wang Shichong's nephew, the false Prince of Dai, Wang Wan, whom Yuwen Shiji had also recognized during the Sui Dynasty. The horse was indeed a fine horse from the imperial stables. The emperor wanted to commend Wei Qing's ability and gave him the horse as a reward.

Emperor Taizong was about to campaign against Goguryeo, but the official known as Wei Gong was ill and unable to accompany him. The emperor summoned high-ranking officials to urge him to comply, but the official refused. The emperor said, "I understand." The next day, the emperor visited the official's residence and bid him farewell with a handshake. The official said, "An old minister should accompany the emperor, but my illness, like that of a dog or horse, has worsened day by day. I fear I may die on the way and disgrace the emperor." The emperor patted his back and said, "Encourage yourself." He then said, "In the past, Sima Yi was not old and ill, yet he was able to strengthen himself and establish merit for the state of Wei." The official knelt and said, "Please allow me to be carried while ill, but I reached Xiangzhou, where my illness became severe and I could not continue." During the campaign, Goguryeo and the Mohe people united their forces forty miles away. Emperor Taizong had a fearful expression. Prince Jiangxia respectfully said, "Goguryeo will use all its forces to resist the emperor's army, and the defense of Pyongyang must be weak. Please allow me to send 5,000 elite soldiers to destroy their core, and then a million troops will surrender without a fight." In the second year of the Jianzhong era, the capital and the Jianghuai region borrowed money and goods from merchants. In the third year of the Jianzhong era, one official was reduced in each state and county. In the fourth year of the Qianyuan era, an imperial decree was issued to reduce officials within a quota, and in the sixth year of the Yuanhe era, officials in states and counties were further reduced. In the fourth year of the Jianzhong era, an imperial decree was issued to all states and counties regarding market transactions, requiring a tax of 50 coins per 1,000 coins to the government. In the fourth year of the Jianzhong era, the Ministry of Revenue submitted a report that a tax on buildings in all states and counties would be imposed with varying rates.

Monk Yihang, during the Kaiyuan era, once presented to Emperor Xuanzong, saying, "Your Majesty's travels ten thousand miles signify the eternal prosperity of the imperial lineage." During the Tianbao era, when the emperor traveled to the eastern capital, nearly ten thousand people were present. When the emperor traveled to Sichuan and reached Wannli Bridge, he realized something. During the Tianbao era, when the country was at peace, those with graceful and beautiful appearances from the six palaces were selected, and the Flower and Bird Envoy was named to host banquets. When Emperor Xuanzong traveled to Sichuan, the eight steeds in the imperial stables, seven of which died on the mountain path, only one, Yunzui, survived. Emperor Dezong traveled to Liang, and it also served as his mount. Below the northern peak of Wutai Mountain is the Qinglong Pool, about two acres in size. According to Buddhist scriptures, it is a place where five hundred venomous dragons are confined. At noon, the fog temporarily clears, allowing monks and lay practitioners to see it once. If a woman approaches, there will be thunder, lightning, and storms, and if she gets close to the pool, she will be sucked into the toxic gases and disappear. Wei Xian Gong Xia Qing did not pass through military districts but only remained in the eastern capital as the governor, with eight county officials under him. Lu Gong Sui Huangfu Yizhou Tui all became prime ministers. Zhang Shangshu, Jia Duan, Geshi Pingzhong, Weidaifu Zhongli, Li Changshi, Li Jianyi Jingjian, and Li Hunan Ci all became high-ranking officials, also known for recognizing talent. Li Xi Tai Wenxian Gong took refuge in the Qinglong Temple to escape the heat and dreamed of a white-haired divine being who said, "In the past, Yin helped King Xuan achieve a revival. Your son will also assist in a revival. It is appropriate to name him Ji Fu." Li Xiangguo Zhong Gong was the governor of Raozhou in the nineteenth year of the Zhenyuan era. Previously, the four old government offices in the east of the city had been abandoned for seven years. After he arrived, he ordered the doors to be opened and moved in. The county officials strongly requested due to strange occurrences. The monk said, "The spirit is indeed righteous. If it is righteous, the spirit will avoid evil and virtue will prevail. If virtue is lost, evil will arise. It depends on the people." This is recorded in the *Tangshu*.

钦定四库全书 子部十二 大唐载 小説家一【杂事之属】提要 【臣】等谨案大唐载一巻不着撰人名氏记唐初至元和中杂事唐宋艺文志俱不载前有自序称八年夏南行岭峤暇日泷舟其所闻而载之考穆宗以后惟太和大中咸通乃有八年此书既不着其纪元之号所云八年者亦不知其在何时也所録唐公卿事迹言论颇详多为史所采用间及于诙谐谈谑及朝野琐事亦往往与他説部相出入惟称贞元中郑国韩国二公主加諡为公主追諡之始而不书髙祖女平阳昭公主有諡已在前又萧頴士逢一老人谓其似鄱阳王据博异记乃巨盗而此纪之以为异人如此与诸书颇不合盖当时流互异作者承所闻而録之故不免抵牾要其可据者实十之六七云乾隆四十四年六月恭校上

总纂官【臣】纪昀【臣】陆锡熊【臣】孙士毅 总 校 官 【臣】 陆 费 墀 钦定四库全书 大唐载 书云不有博奕者乎犹贤乎已斯圣人疾夫饱食而怠惰之深也又曰吾不试故艺试用也夫艺者不独总多能苐以其无用于代而穷愁时有所述耳八年夏南行极岭峤暇日泷舟传其所闻而载之故曰载虽小説或有可观览之而喁而笑焉 杜河南兼常聚书至万卷每卷后必有自题云清俸买来手自校汝曹读之知圣道鬻及借人为不孝 阳道州城之为朝士也家苦贫常以木枕布衾质钱数万人争取之 苏州开元寺东有陆氏世居门临河涘有巨石块立焉乃呉陆绩为郁林郡守罢秩泛海而归不载寳货舟轻用此石重之人号郁林石陆氏自绩及裔孙国朝太子少保兖公犹保其居今子孙渐削其居十不存一焉费县西漏泽者漫十数里嵗时雨降即泛溢自满蒲鱼之利人实赖焉至白露应节前后一夕即一空如扫焉信殊异也

李忠公之为相也政事堂有防食之床吏人相移之则宰臣罢不迁者五十年公曰朝夕论道之所岂可使朽蠧之物而不除俗言拘忌何足聴也以此获免余之愿焉命彻而焚其下铲去聚壤十四畚议者称正焉杜太保宣简公大厯中有故人遗黄金百两后三十年为淮南节度使其子投公取其黄金还缄封如故赵郡三祖元和中每房一人同时为相皆第三即司徒吉甫司空绛华州刺史藩 天寳中有书生旅次宋州时汧公勉少年贫苦与书生同店而不旬日书生疾作遂至不救临絶语公曰某家住洪州将于北都求官于此得疾且死其命也因出金百两付公曰某之仆使无知有此者足下为我毕死事余金奉之李公许为办事及毕宻置金于墓中而同葬焉后数年公尉开封书生兄弟赍洪州牒来果然寻生行止至宋州知李为

主丧事专诣开封诘金之所公请假至墓所以出金付之焉韦献公夏卿有知人之鉴人不知也因退朝于街中逄再从弟执谊从弟渠牟升三人皆第二十四并为郎官簇马良久献公曰今日逄三二十四郎輙欲题目之语执谊曰汝必为宰相善保其末耳语渠牟曰弟当别奉主上恩而速贵为公卿语升曰三人之中弟最长远而位极旄后竟如其言 杜亚为淮南竞渡采莲龙舟锦缆绣防之戏费金数千万于頔为襄州防山灯一上油二千石李昌防为荆南打猎大修富饰其妻独孤氏亦出女队二千人皆着红紫锦绣袄子此三府亦因而空耗 汝南徳师故给事高之子尝于东都买得娄师徳故园地起书楼洛人语曰昔日娄师徳园今乃徳师楼兴元元年十月戊辰始诏中官窦文监神防军左厢兵马马有麟为左神防大将军神策监军军之始也贞元十二年六月乙丑始以窦文为左神防防军中尉霍仙鸣为右神防防军中尉其日又以张尚进为神武中防军左右辟仗使之始也

建中初关播为给事中以诸司胥吏为颇多播议用士人掌之 农杨氏居东都者承四太尉之后世传黄雀所衔玉环至天寳为杨国忠所夺今不知所在 张守珪陜州平陆人自幽州入觐过本县见令李杭申桑梓之礼见陜尉李冕桎梏令众寃呼张公曰困危之中岂能相救至灵寳便奏兖州判官冕后至宰相贞元中张茂宗所尚义章公主赠郑国公主諡为庄穆韦宥所尚故唐安公主赠韩国公主諡为贞穆所司择日册命国朝已来公主即有追封者未有加諡者公主追諡自此始也 徐尚书晦沈吏部师徐公嗜酒沈公善食杨东川嗣复尝云徐家肺沈家脾真安穏 有士人平生好食熝牛头一日忽梦其物故拘至地府酆都狱有牛头在旁其人了无畏惮仍以手抚其头云只者头子大堪熝食牛头人笑而放回 元和中郎吏数人省中纵酒语平生各爱尚及憎怕者或言爱图画及博奕怕妄与佞工部员外汝南周愿独云爱宣州观察使怕大虫

贞元中禁未仕不得乘大马有人言于执政大马甚多货不得举人不得骑当尽为河北节制所得耳 陆鸿渐嗜茶撰茶经三行于代常见鬻茶邸烧瓦瓷为其形貌置于灶釜上左右为茶神有交易则茶祭之无则以釡汤沃之 高祖之制凡出将赐旌节专征行军有大总管之号也高祖之制镇军有大都督之号 宗之初为节度大使节度之号凡皇太子亲王统军中有元帅府之制宰相遥领节度使自牛仙客始也开元九年命宗正寺官寮并以宗子为之唐制男子始生为黄四嵗为小十六为中二十为丁六十为老赋役之制有四一曰租二曰税三曰役四曰徭凡丁嵗输粟二石凡调随乡土所产绢绫絁各二丈布加五分之一麻三斤凡役嵗二旬闰加二日 至徳元年三月方以侍御史文叔清为宣谕使许人纳钱授官及明经出身 至徳二年勅以僧及道士入钱自度有差 乾元元年七月铸钱使第五琦奏谓铸乾元钱毎贯重二十斤一文当五十寳应元年以盗铸日甚物价腾贵咸称非便减重轮钱以一当三十

乾元二年御史中丞元载为江淮五道租庸使高户定数徴钱谓之白着酤 至徳二年勅天下州县量定酤酒随月纳税建中二年更加青苖大厯中初税毎亩十文三年加五文勅以御史大夫充使其后割归度支 卢中丞迈有寳琴四各直数十万有寒玉古磬响泉和志之号李河南素替杜公兼时韩吏部愈为河南令除职方员外归朝问前后之政如何对曰将兼来比素 李相国程执政时严休皆在南省有万年令阙人多属之李公云二严不如誉 豆卢署少年旅于衢州梦老人云君后二十年为牧兹郡已果为衢州于所梦之地立徴梦亭 郑滁州胪于曲江见令史醉池岸云更一转即入流矣白宾客居易云忠州有荔枝一株槐一株自忠之南更无槐自忠之北更无荔枝 贾至常侍平生毁佛尝假寐防事忽见一牛首人长不满尺擕小锅而燃薪于床前公惊起而讯之对曰所谓镬汤者罪其毁佛人公曰小何足畏遂伸足床下其汤沸忽染于足涌然而上未防烘烂而卒

乌江有项羽系乌骓树歴千余年尚郁茂建中年中县令张勤厌宾客观逰令伐却至今兹地独不生草李镇恶即赵公峤之父选校梓州郪县令与友人书云州带子号县带妻名由来不属老夫并是妇児官职刘巨麟开元中为广州刺史弟仲丘为丽政殿学士兄弟友爱有罗浮道者为巨麟合丹剂将分半以遗仲丘命刀中破之分铢无差焉 萧功曹頴士赵员外骥开元中同居兴敬里肄业共一靴久而见东郭之迹赵曰可谓驶于道路矣萧曰无乃禄在其中 苏州洞庭杭州兴徳寺房太尉琯云不逰兴徳洞庭未见山水 寿安县有喷玉泉石溪皆山水之胜絶也贞元中李宾客洞为县令乃刬翳荟开径隧人方闻而异焉太和初博陵崔为主簿标堠于道周人方造而游焉 顔太师鲁公刻名于石或置之髙山之上或沉之大洲之底而云安知不陵谷之变

独孤常州及末年尤嗜鼓琴得眼疾不理意欲专聴也曲阜县先圣庙前有数株古栢亦千余嵗其大十围潘华为兖州军食贫穷无以结四方之信华遂命伐之裁为简册刻为器皿以行饷之 张文贞公第某女嫁卢氏尝为舅卢公求官公朝下而问焉公不语但指搘床而示之女拜而归室告其夫曰舅得事矣 开元东封有太原人于伯陇者年一百二十八嵗精爽不昧其子已卒两孙随之各年七八十矣自北乗诣阙引见上劳之老人无拜礼伯陇曰臣神尧皇帝之臣也荏苒嵗月得至今日复事郎君臣之幸矣郎君明圣功成封岳不以昏老千里而来笑而悯之乃赐紫袍牙笏及优防有加伯陇自言隋仁寿年生説大业末事了然可见李右丞廙年二十九为尚书右丞至五十九又为尚书右丞于良史为张徐州建封事但举其姓曰辛丘杜当人萧功曹颖士尝出灞桥道左逄一老人眉髪皓白状骨甚竒古萧甚异之老人瞻顾萧因问之老人云公似吾亡友耳萧固请言之老人曰吾与鄱阳王恢善君甚之乃頴士六代祖萧问其所来不应而去
洛阳金谷去城二十五里晋石崇依金谷为园苑髙台飞阁余址嶙独有一皂防树甚大至今郁茂郑公审开元中为殿中侍御史充馆驿使令每舎立辰堠自公始也 润州金坛县大厯中北人为主簿以竹筒赍蝎十余枚置于防事之栁树后遂孳育至百余枚为土气所蒸而不能螫人南民不识呼为主簿虫 开元中进士第唱于尚书省其防试者并集于都堂唱其第于尚书省有落去者两两三三戴帽子日暮但吟一声长安竹帛皆枯死

开元中吏部侍郎宁王宪嘱亲故十人官遂诣王请见云十人之中有商量去者乎王云九人皆不可矣一人某者聴公吏部归九人皆超资好官独某者当时出云据其书判自合得官縁嘱宁王且放冬集 昆山县遗尺潭本大厯中村女为皇太子元妃遗玉尺化为龙至今遂成潭 洛东龙门香山寺上方则天时名望春宫则天常御石楼坐朝文武百执事班于外而朝焉 永和中有判太常寺行事礼官祭圜丘至时不到云太常大寺实曰伽蓝圜丘小僧不合无礼 沙堤起天寳三年因萧京兆炅奉请于要路筑甬道以通车骑而覆沙其上 天寳中乐章名以边地为名若凉州甘州伊州之是焉其曲遍繁声名入破后其地尽为西番所没其破兆矣 高平徐毅为弹侍御史创一知班官令自宣政门检朝官之失仪者到台司举而罚焉有公卿大僚令问之曰未到班行之中何必拾人细事毅报之曰为我谢公卿所以然者以恶其无礼于其君

裴仆射遵庆二十入仕裹折上巾子未尝随俗様凡代之移易者五六而公年九十所裹者犹幼小时様今巾子有仆射様 韩太保皋生知音律尝观客弹琴为止息乃叹曰妙哉嵇生者也为是曲也其当魏晋之际止息与广陵散同出而异者也其音主商商为秋声天将肃杀草木摇落其嵗之晏乎此所以为魏之季也慢其商徳与宫同音是臣夺其君之位乎此所以知司马氏之将簒也广陵维之地散者流亡之谓也者武帝后之姓也言杨后与其父骏之倾覆晋祚也止息者晋虽兴终止息于此其音哀愤而噍杀操蹙而憯痛永嘉之乱其应乎此叔夜撰此将贻后代知音且避晋祸托之鬼神史氏非知味者安得不传其谬也欤 韩太保皋常言洪范五福独不言贵者近于高危福之自至犹儆动奈何枉道邀之 李西平晟之将军也尝谒桑道茂云将军异日为京兆尹慎少杀人西平曰武夫岂有京兆尹望乎后兴元复西平兼京兆时道茂在俘囚中当断之际告西平曰公忘少杀人之言乎

韩太保皋之为御史中丞京兆尹常有所陈必于紫宸对百僚而请未尝诣便殿上谓曰我与卿言于此不尽可来延英议及大政多匡益之亲友咸谓公曰自乾元以来羣臣启事皆诣延英方得详尽公何独于外庭对众官以陈之得无不慎宻乎公曰御史天下之平也摧刚直枉惟在公何在不可人知之奈何求请便殿避人窃语以私国家之法且延英之置也肃宗皇帝以苗晋卿年老艰步故设之后来得诣便殿多以私自售希防求宠干求相位奈何以此为望哉 张秘书荐自筮仕至秘书监常带使职三入蕃竟殁于赤岭外 韦崖州执自幼不喜闻岭南州县拜相日出外舎见一州郡图迟回不敢防良乆临起悮视乃崖州图也竟以贬终 王河南维或有人报云公除右辖王曰吾居此官虑被人呼为不解作诗王右丞 阳道州城未尝有所蓄积惟所服用不可阙者客称其物可佳可爱公辄喜举而授之有陈苌者候其始请月俸常往称其钱帛之美月有获者

常中书处原在开州也常有李潼崔冲二进士来谒留连月余日防有过客西川军将某能相术于席上言李潼三日内有虎厄后三日相君与诸客逰山寺自上方抵下方日已暮矣李先下崔后来冲大呼李云待冲来待冲来李闻待冲来声谓虎至矣颠蹶坠下山址絶而复苏数日方愈及军将回谓李曰君厄过矣 苏给事岱建中末为太常博士遇朱泚乱将赴行在夜行山谷常有二烛前导危险毕见既过烛然后灭岂忠愤所感耶 李年彭年鹤年兄弟三人开元中皆有才学盛名鹤年诗尤妙唱渭城彭年善舞年善打羯鼓宗问卿打多少杖对曰臣打五千杖讫上曰汝殊未我打却三竖柜也后数年有闻打一竖柜因赐一拂杖羯皷后卷流至建中三年任使君又一弟子使君今取江陵漆盘底泻水卷中竟日不散以其至平又云卷人鼓只在调竖慢此卷一调之后经月如初今不知所存安邑县北门县人云有一蝎如琵琶大毎出来不毒人人由是恐其灵闭之积年矣

泾州将郝玭自贞元末及元和中数于泾州擒杀西虏及筑临泾城西戎畏之赞普铸一金郝玭号曰有能得玭者赐金玭焉 建中中李希烈攻汴州城防用百姓妇女辎重以实壕堑谓之湿稍 窦参之作相也用从父弟申为耳目毎除吏先言于申申告人故谓窦给事为喜鹊 同州唐女寺有盗帅董太宫之漆身后有盗者皆来拜祝有至鸣足者今漆足皆口牙 襄州汉臯庙本为解佩于汉之义今为汉高祖误也陜州平陆县主簿防事西序楣有隋房公杜公仁寿九年十二月题龄如晦题处房年二十三杜年二十六今移在使府食堂之梁

杨京兆凭兄弟二人皆能文学甚攻苦或同赋一篇共坐庭石霜积襟袖课成乃已李愿司空兄弟九人四有土地愿为夏州徐泗凤翔宣武河中五节度宪为江西观察岭南节度愬为唐邓襄阳徐泗凤翔泽潞魏博六节度使聴为夏州灵武河东郑滑魏博分宁凤翔七节度一门登坛授无比焉 元和十五年辛丘度丘纾杜元頴同时为拾遗令史分直故事每自吟日出身三十年髪白衣仍碧日暮倚朱门从朱汗赤因为之奏章服焉 河南广武山有流桂泉史思明于其上立汉高庙茅山下泊宫茅君链丹井香洁不受触曽有修宫工人获知取水煮肉良久不熟 寳厯中亳州云出圣水服之愈宿疾亦无一差者自洛已来及江西数郡中人争施金货衣服以饮焉获利千万人转相惑李赞皇徳在浙西也命于大市集人置金取其水于市司取猪肉五斤煮云若圣水也肉当如故逡巡肉熟烂自此人心稍定妖者寻而败露

朗州武陵山有人换骨匣每年若大风雨望峭壁即有新者泽州长平即白起坑赵卒故地开元中诏为省寃谷至今天气长隂有泉色赤于其下立丹井碑 湖州徳清县南前溪村前朝教乐舞之地今尚有数百家尽习音乐江南声妓多自此出所谓舞出前溪者也终南山有湫池本咸阳大洲一夜忽飞去所歴皆暴雨与鱼俱下大者至四五尺小者不可胜计遂落终南山中峰水浮数尺纵广一里余色如黛黒云雨常自中出焦旱祈祷无不应焉山僧采樵时见羣龙瀺灂其中昭应庆山长安中从河朔飞来夜过闻雷声如疾风土石乱下直坠新丰西一村百余家因山为坟今于其上起持国寺 魏齐公元中少时曽谒张憬藏待之甚薄就质通塞亦不答公大怒曰仆不逺千里裹粮求见非徒行也意必谓明公有以见教而含木舌不尽勤勤之意何耶且穷通贫贱自属苍苍何预公焉因拂衣而去憬藏遽起言曰君之相禄正在怒中后位极人臣 解县盐池当安史时水忽淡銮舆反正复如故

上都崇圣寺有徐贤妃妆殿太宗曾召妃乆不至怒之因进诗曰朝来临镜台妆罢暂徘徊千金始一笑一召讵能来 汉中王瑀为太常卿早起朝闻永兴里人吹笛问是太常乐人否曰然已后因闻乐而挞之问曰何得某日卧吹笛又见康昆仑弹琵琶云琵声多琶声少亦未可弹五十四丝大也自下而上谓之琵自上而下谓之琶 裴延龄尝怒李京兆充云近日兼放髭须白犹向人前作背靣 薛汝丹家在南岳常与一僧知闻其僧毎年以香链顶供飬佛至八十余终后嵗余有州民生一子色貌絶殊而顶甚香十步之外人皆慕之生不食鱼肉数嵗出家为南岳高行律师焉 王藻王素贞元初应举名皆第十四每诣通家通王十四郎或问曰藻也素也 常相衮为礼部判杂文榜后云旭日登塲思非不鋭通宵絶笔恨即有余所以杂文入者常不过百人鲍祭酒防为礼部帖经落人亦甚时谓之常杂鲍帖 左右常侍与给谏同厨厨人进鲜菌于给谏问云莫毒否厨人答曰常侍已尝了

乾元中太子左赞善大夫吕旬母张氏年八十八以旬久不归愈思念之忽觉左乳长汁流出顾谓孙鄢汝父即来也不逾十日旬遂至 至徳初当安史乱河东大饥忽然荒地十五里生豆及扫却又复生约得五六千石其米甚圆细复美人赖焉开元天宝之间传家法者崔沔崔均之家法 师云长安永宁坊东南是金盏地安邑里西是玉盏地永宁为王太傅锷地安邑为马北平燧地后王马皆进入官王宅累赐韩令及史宪诚李戴义等所谓金盏破而成焉马燧为奉诚园所谓玉破而不完也 常相衮之在福建也有僧某者善占色言事若神相国惜其僧老命弟子就学其术僧云此事有天性非可造次为某尝于相君左右见一人可教遍召得小吏黄彻焉相命就学老僧遂于闇室中置五色彩于架令视之曰世人皆用眼力不尽淬熟防之旬后依稀认其白者后半载防五色即洞然而得矣命之曰以闇中之视五彩回之白昼占人因传其方诀且言后代当无加也李忠公吉甫云黄彻之占袁许之亚次也

礼部刘尚书禹锡与友人三年同处其友人云未尝见刘公説重话 唐公临性寛仁多慈恕欲吊丧令家僮归取白衫僮仆悮持余衣惧未敢进临祭公谓之曰今日气逆不宜哀泣向取白衫且止之又令煮药不精潜觉其故又谓曰今日隂晦不宜服药可弃之终不其过矣 唐皎贞观中为吏部先时选集四时随到即补皎始请以冬时大集终季春而毕至今行之 徐大理宥少为蒲州司法叅军为政寛仁吏感其恩信逓相约曰若犯徐司法杖必斥罚终官不杖一人顔鲁公真卿为监察御史充河西陇右军覆屯交兵使五原有寃狱决乃雨郡人呼为御史雨

徳宗问李汧公勉人云卢杞是奸邪何也勉曰人皆知之陛下独不知此所以为奸邪也 李希烈防扈蔡州时卢杞为相奏顔鲁公往宣谕之而谓顔曰十三丈此行出自圣意顔曰公先中丞面上血某亲舌防之乃忍以垂死之年饵于虎口杞闻之啼焉卢即是御史中丞奕之子 南蛮清平官犹国家之宰相也元和中有邓旁来庭宰臣问之公名旁其何意乎对曰亦犹大朝之刘宗经矣苏户部弁刘常侍伯刍皆聚书至二万卷河南冯宿之三子陶鞠图兄弟连年进士及第连年登宏词科一时之盛代无比焉当太和初冯氏进士及第者海内十八而公家兄弟叔侄八人 李相国程为翰林学士以堦塼日影为入公性懒每入必逾八塼故号为八塼学士焉 永州龙兴寺乃呉军司马之故宅僧懐素善草尝浚井得军司马印文字不灭雕刻如新懐素每草书用此为志 英公虽贵为仆射其姊病必亲为粥火燃輙焚其髭姊曰仆妾多矣何为自苦勣曰岂无人顾今姊年老勣亦年老虽欲乆为姊粥复可得乎

英公尝言我年十二三时为无赖贼逢人则杀十四五时为难当贼有所不惬者杀之十七八时为好贼上阵杀人年二十便为天下大将军用兵以救人死 尉迟敬徳性饶寛而尤善避槊每军骑入阵敌人刺之终不能中反夺其槊以刺敌人海陵王元吉闻之不信乃令去槊刃以试焉敬徳曰饶王着刃亦不畏伤元吉再三来刺既不少中而槊皆被夺去元吉力敌十夫大慙恨太宗之御窦建徳谓尉迟公曰寡人持弓箭公把长鎗二人相副虽百万众亦无奈乃与敬徳驰至敌营叩其军门大呼曰大唐秦王能敌来与汝决追骑甚众不敢御 窦建徳之役既阵未战太宗见一少年骑骢马铠甲鲜明指谓尉迟公曰彼所乗马真良马也言之不已敬徳请取之帝曰轻敌者亡脱以一马损公非寡人愿敬徳自料攻之万全乃驰往并擒少年而返即王世充之兄子伪代王琬宇文士及在隋亦识之是马实内廏之良马也帝欲旌其能并以赐之

太宗将征辽卫公病不能从帝使执政已下起之不起帝曰吾知之矣明日驾临其第执手与别靖曰老臣宜从但犬马之疾日月増甚恐死于道路仰累陛下帝抚其背曰勉之昔司马仲逹非不老病竟能自强立勲魏室靖叩头曰请舆病行至相州疾笃不能进 驻跸之役高丽与靺鞨合军四十里太宗有惧色江夏王进曰髙丽倾国以拒王师平壤之守必弱请假臣精卒五千覆其本根则千万之众不战而降 借商建中二年京师及江淮借商钱物 省官建中三年天下州县各省一官乾元四年勑下注额内官元和六年又减州县官 除陌建中四年勑天下州县市买交关毎贯五十文纳官间架建中四年戸部侍郎赵瓒奏天下州县屋宇间架率算钱有差

沙门一行开元中尝奏宗云陛下行幸万里圣祚无疆故天寳中幸东都庶盈万数及上幸蜀至万里桥方悟焉天寳中天下无事选六宫风流艶态者名花鸟使主宴宗幸蜀天廏八骏其七尽毙于栈道惟一云骓存焉徳宗幸梁亦充御焉 五台山北台下有青龙池约二亩已来佛经云禁五百毒龙之所每至盛午昏雾暂开比丘及净行居士方可一观比丘尼及女子近即雷电风雨当时大作如近池必为毒气所吸逡巡而没 韦献公夏卿不经方镇唯止于东都留守郡吏八人而路公隋皇甫崖州鏄皆为宰相张尚书贾叚给事平仲卫大夫中行李常侍翺李谏议景俭李湖南词皆至显官亦名知人矣 李西台文献公避暑于青龙寺梦戴白神人云昔尹氏相宣王致中兴君男亦佐中兴君宜以吉甫名之李相国忠公贞元十九年为饶州刺史先是郡城之东四牧故府废者七稔公涖止后命启籥而居之郡吏以语怪坚请公曰神实正正则神避妖不胜徳失徳则妖兴居之在人 大唐载
